---
title: "About Me"
---

## About Me

I’m a Master of Environmental Data Science candidate at UC Santa Barbara with a background in GHG accounting, environmental analytics, and sustainability reporting. My path into environmental data work began during my AmeriCorps service with Honolulu’s Office of Climate Change, Sustainability and Resiliency, where I co-developed the methodology for the city’s first countywide GHG inventory and contributed to its inaugural Sustainability Report and Resilience Strategy.

Before AmeriCorps, I served in the Peace Corps in Georgia, where I supported a local NGO by modernizing its digital and data systems. I built the organization’s website, developed a database to track youth program participation, and taught Excel and introductory Python to students. Working in a resource-limited environment strengthened my adaptability, problem-solving skills, and ability to design practical, accessible data tools — qualities that continue to shape my approach to environmental analytics today.

## Technical Skills & Tools

As a Masters Student I analyze environmental and Geospatial data using R and Python. Packages that I use in my workflow include:

| Library | Description |
|------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `tidyverse` | data wrangling and plotting |
| `terra` | raster processing, reclassification, resampling, map algebra |
| `sf` | reading shapefiles, managing projections, spatial joins |
| `stars` | converts rasters for use with tmap and ggplot2 |
| `tmap` | creates maps with basemaps, legends, labels, scale bars, compasses |
| `knitr` / `kableExtra` | table formatting and report‑ready tables |
| `geopandas` | load shapefiles, reproject CRS, clip vectors, spatial joins |
| `xarray` | open NetCDFs, access spectral bands, convert to arrays |
| `matplotlib` | true/false‑color composites, fire overlays, raster visualization |
| `pandas` | tabular data manipulation |
| `numpy` | numerical operations, masks, array calculations |

## What I do for fun

Outside of data work, I enjoy film photography, cooking, playing guitar, and taking my Volkswagen camping. I also collect vinyl records and love exploring new cuisines — especially Thai food (drunken noodles, larb, and yellow curry) and Japanese ramen, with a special love for tsukemen.

![](photos/turkiye/turk_breakfast.jpg){.gallery-img}
